company.name: Summit Restoration & Reconstruction
company.description: Summit Restoration & Reconstruction is a full-service property damage restoration and general contracting firm headquartered in Irving, TX, serving the Dallas-Fort Worth metroplex. The company handles the full claim lifecycle — from emergency mitigation through structural reconstruction — primarily for residential and light commercial insurance losses. Founded by a former State Farm-preferred contractor, Summit operates 9 mitigation crews and 4 reconstruction crews across 3 service territories in DFW.

xactanalysis.notes: High denial rate traced to inconsistent line item documentation and missing scope notes on field estimates.

estimation.sketch_tool: Xactimate built-in sketch (not using XactSketch mobile)
pain_points: Estimators build scopes on laptops in the field then re-enter into desktop Xactimate back at the office — double entry on every job
No real-time collaboration — 5 estimators email .esx files and overwrite each other
Supplement denial rate at 31% — adjusters cite 'missing documentation' and 'unsupported line items'
Cannot attach moisture logs or drying reports directly to the Xactimate file
Sketch is slow on large commercial losses — estimators report 2–3 hours per floor plan
No audit trail when scope is revised after adjuster negotiation
No integration with Xactimate — job data is manually re-keyed
Crew scheduling is done in a shared Google Calendar outside the system
Paper logs frequently lost or illegible — creates documentation gaps on supplement support
MoistureMapper adoption is partial — only 3 of 9 crews use it consistently

documentation_and_photo.notes: CompanyCam used for photo documentation but not integrated with Xactimate — estimators manually copy photo links into scope notes

current_bottlenecks: Estimate scope documentation quality is inconsistent across 5 estimators — no standardized template
Supplements are written by the PM, not the original estimator — context is lost between handoffs
Insurer adjuster negotiations happen over email with no version control on scope revisions
Reconstruction job costing is manual — no live budget vs. actual tracking

key_contacts.name: Marcus Delray
key_contacts.title: Owner / President
key_contacts.notes: Founded Summit in 2008 after 7 years as a State Farm preferred contractor. Operationally focused. Primary concern is margin — specifically supplement recovery and reducing the cost of re-doing estimates. Attends RIA Annual every year. Active in RIA forums. Posted in April 2026: 'Anyone actually improved their supplement approval rate without hiring more people?'

key_contacts.name: Priya Nair
key_contacts.title: Operations Manager
key_contacts.notes: Manages estimator scheduling, XactAnalysis submissions, and TPA compliance. Day-to-day pain point owner. Likely the internal champion if she believes TrueRestore reduces her rework load. Has direct visibility into the 31% supplement denial rate.

key_contacts.name: Derek Okafor
key_contacts.title: Lead Estimator
key_contacts.notes: 10 years in restoration estimating, Xactimate-certified at Level 3. Manages the 4 junior estimators. Vocal internally about double-entry pain. Viewed TrueRestore product page on 2026-06-08 and 2026-06-19. Likely an internal advocate if he sees a workflow improvement.

key_contacts.name: Tamara Wells
key_contacts.title: Reconstruction Project Manager
key_contacts.notes: Manages the 4 reconstruction crews and subcontractor relationships. Frustrated with the Xactimate-to-Dash handoff gap. Would benefit from better scope-to-budget traceability.

signals.signal: Derek Okafor (Lead Estimator) visited TrueRestore.com/pricing twice in the last 30 days.
Summit posted 2 Field Estimator job listings on Indeed — scaling estimating capacity, which increases the cost of bad tooling.
Marcus Delray posted in RIA Community Forum: 'Anyone actually improved their supplement approval rate without hiring more people?' — 14 replies, active thread.
Attended RIA Annual Conference 2026 in Nashville — AE Jordan Malone made initial contact on-site.
Travelers contractor program up for renewal in Q3 2026 — Summit needs clean XactAnalysis submission history to qualify. Current 31% denial rate is a risk.

why_they_stayed_on_xactimate: Carrier requirement — State Farm and Allstate mandate Xactimate-formatted estimates for large losses
Team is fully trained — retraining cost is perceived as high
No internal champion had time to run a proper evaluation
likely_objections: Carriers require Xactimate output — can TrueRestore export compliant .esx files?
We have 5 estimators trained on Xactimate — what's the retraining burden?
Show me the ROI — specifically what does this do to our supplement denial rate?
Will this work inside XactAnalysis SP for our TPA submissions?
trurestore_differentiators_to_lead_with: Xactimate-compatible output — no carrier workflow disruption
Mobile-first field capture eliminates double entry (field → desktop re-key)
Scope standardization templates reduce the inconsistency driving the 31% denial rate
Integrated moisture log and drying report attachment — closes the biggest supplement documentation gap
Audit trail on scope revisions — supports adjuster negotiation with version history

firmographic_score.rationale: 72-person full-service restoration firm, $14M ARR, insurance-driven (78% of revenue), active on 4 TPA programs, 5 Xactimate seats, 31% supplement denial rate creating direct margin pain, active intent signals from lead estimator, and a carrier renewal deadline creating urgency. Squarely in TrueRestore's ICP.
